exposures.label: NVIDIA Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: NVIDIA dominates the AI accelerator market with its H100/H200/Blackwell GPU architecture. Hyperscaler AI training and inference capital expenditure flows directly to GPU revenue. NVIDIA's data centre segment accounted for the majority of fiscal 2025 revenue.
exposures.risk_note: Export controls and competitive pressure from AMD and custom ASICs are key risks.

exposures.label: Advanced Micro Devices
exposures.reason_to_care: AMD's Instinct MI300X and MI350X GPUs are competing for hyperscaler AI spend. GPU ramp is a direct AI infrastructure beneficiary and AMD provides EPYC CPU exposure to data-centre server buildout.

exposures.label: Broadcom Inc.
exposures.reason_to_care: Broadcom's custom AI ASICs for Google, Meta, and Apple and Tomahawk/Jericho networking chips are critical for AI cluster connectivity. Custom silicon is structural multi-year revenue.

exposures.label: Marvell Technology
exposures.reason_to_care: Marvell custom AI ASICs and optical interconnects benefit from data-centre build-out. Custom silicon ramp for Amazon and other hyperscalers is a growing revenue segment.

exposures.label: Arista Networks
exposures.reason_to_care: Arista provides ultra-low-latency Ethernet switches for AI training cluster back-end networks. AI cluster networking is a direct and growing revenue driver with Microsoft and Meta as major customers.

exposures.label: Super Micro Computer
exposures.reason_to_care: Super Micro's AI server building blocks (rack-scale solutions, direct liquid cooling) directly serve hyperscaler GPU buildout. Revenue tied to GPU server shipment volumes.
exposures.risk_note: Accounting and audit risk has previously created volatility.

exposures.label: Dell Technologies
exposures.reason_to_care: Dell PowerEdge AI servers and infrastructure solutions benefit from enterprise AI buildout, particularly in on-premises AI deployment scenarios.

exposures.label: Vertiv Holdings
exposures.reason_to_care: Vertiv provides power and cooling infrastructure directly for AI data centres. Revenue is directly correlated with data-centre power spend. Management has cited AI as the primary revenue driver.

exposures.label: Eaton Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: Eaton provides power management systems, switchgear, UPS, and data-centre power infrastructure. Revenue directly tied to power infrastructure spend for data centres and grid upgrades.

exposures.label: Quanta Services
exposures.reason_to_care: Quanta installs grid infrastructure, substations, and electrical systems for data centres, utilities, and transmission projects. Growing backlog directly tracks power infrastructure spend.

exposures.label: EMCOR Group
exposures.reason_to_care: EMCOR provides electrical and mechanical construction for data-centre power buildout. Data-centre segment is a growing proportion of revenue with increasing project backlog.

exposures.label: Hubbell Inc.
exposures.reason_to_care: Hubbell manufactures transformers, switchgear, and electrical components for data-centre power infrastructure and grid upgrades.

exposures.label: Constellation Energy
exposures.reason_to_care: Constellation is the largest US nuclear operator and has signed power purchase agreements with AI hyperscalers including Microsoft. Nuclear provides reliable 24/7 carbon-free baseload power uniquely matching data-centre uptime SLAs.

exposures.label: Vistra Corp.
exposures.reason_to_care: Vistra operates nuclear and gas power generation. Nuclear and dispatchable power capacity serves long-term data-centre baseload demand contracts.

exposures.label: NextEra Energy
exposures.reason_to_care: NextEra has nuclear capacity but is primarily a renewables utility. Data-centre power exposure is secondary to regulated utility revenue. Broad utility portfolio dilutes pure-play thesis.
exposures.risk_note: Broad utility — nuclear thesis is diluted.

exposures.label: Cameco Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: Cameco is the world's largest publicly traded uranium producer. Higher nuclear baseload demand for AI power drives uranium fuel demand, supporting long-term contract pricing.

exposures.label: Uranium Energy Corp.
exposures.reason_to_care: Uranium Energy is a US uranium producer. Domestic uranium supply-chain investment and nuclear power demand are direct revenue tailwinds.

exposures.label: Energy Fuels Inc.
exposures.reason_to_care: Energy Fuels is a leading US uranium and rare earth producer. Nuclear revival driver directly supports uranium demand and fuel cycle investment.

exposures.label: Denison Mines
exposures.reason_to_care: Denison Mines is a uranium developer with high-grade Canadian assets. Uranium demand growth benefits development pipeline valuations.

exposures.label: Global X Uranium ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: Global X Uranium ETF provides basket exposure to uranium miners and nuclear fuel cycle companies. Useful for investors seeking diversified uranium supply chain exposure.

exposures.label: Sprott Uranium Miners ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: Sprott Uranium Miners ETF provides concentrated exposure to pure-play uranium companies. Higher uranium purity than URA.

exposures.label: Centrus Energy Corp.
exposures.reason_to_care: Centrus Energy is the only US company licensed to produce HALEU (High-Assay Low-Enriched Uranium), the fuel required by most advanced reactor designs including SMRs. DOE has awarded production contracts, and the Piketon facility has reached the 900kg production mark.

exposures.label: ASP Isotopes
exposures.reason_to_care: ASP Isotopes has technology related to uranium enrichment. Lacks direct HALEU production evidence confirmed by official sources.
exposures.risk_note: Needs official source confirmation before active classification.

exposures.label: BWX Technologies
exposures.reason_to_care: BWX Technologies manufactures nuclear reactors and components for the US Navy and commercial nuclear programs. SMR reactor component manufacturing and government nuclear services are structural revenue.

exposures.label: GE Vernova
exposures.reason_to_care: GE Vernova provides power generation equipment including nuclear services and industrial gas turbines. Nuclear services and grid equipment benefit from the AI power demand cycle.

exposures.label: NuScale Power
exposures.reason_to_care: NuScale Power's SMR design is the first NRC-certified SMR design in the US. Catalyst-driven with commercial revenue timeline uncertainty. Benefit from nuclear revival narrative.
exposures.risk_note: Development stage; commercial revenue timeline is highly uncertain.

exposures.label: VanEck Uranium & Nuclear ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: VanEck Uranium & Nuclear ETF provides exposure to uranium mining, nuclear power generation, and nuclear equipment companies across the full nuclear value chain.

exposures.label: Freeport-McMoRan
exposures.reason_to_care: Freeport-McMoRan is the world's largest publicly traded copper producer. AI data-centre construction and grid expansion both require significant copper for wiring, transformers, and electrical systems.
exposures.risk_note: Note: also appears in critical_minerals_copper theme.

exposures.label: Global X Copper Miners ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: Global X Copper Miners ETF provides basket exposure to global copper mining companies.

exposures.label: Eli Lilly
exposures.reason_to_care: Eli Lilly's Zepbound (tirzepatide) and Mounjaro are leading approved GLP-1 drugs. US Zepbound revenue grew 122% year-over-year in Q4 2025 per Lilly investor relations. Primary pharmaceutical revenue from obesity and diabetes treatment.
exposures.risk_note: Valuation and competitive pipeline risk.

exposures.label: Novo Nordisk
exposures.reason_to_care: Novo Nordisk's Ozempic and Wegovy (semaglutide) are the originator GLP-1 drugs with global manufacturing scale. Novo 2025 annual report shows strong GLP-1 and obesity-care sales with manufacturing investment as a key constraint.
exposures.risk_note: Manufacturing capacity and competitive pipeline are key variables.

exposures.label: West Pharmaceutical Services
exposures.reason_to_care: West Pharmaceutical Services makes injectable drug delivery systems — the stoppers and closures for GLP-1 drug vials and cartridges. GLP-1 drug volume growth directly increases West's container demand.

exposures.label: Thermo Fisher Scientific
exposures.reason_to_care: Thermo Fisher provides pharmaceutical manufacturing services including peptide API synthesis and fill-finish capacity critical for GLP-1 scale-up through its pharma services segment.

exposures.label: Danaher Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: Danaher's bioprocessing and life sciences tools support GLP-1 drug manufacturing and quality control through its Cytiva and Pall biotech brands.

exposures.label: Becton Dickinson
exposures.reason_to_care: Becton Dickinson manufactures prefilled syringes and auto-injector pens used for GLP-1 drug delivery. Device volume tied directly to GLP-1 prescription growth.

exposures.label: DexCom
exposures.reason_to_care: DexCom makes continuous glucose monitors (CGMs). GLP-1 adoption improves metabolic management but may reduce insulin-dependent CGM use over time. Mixed exposure — monitor for direction.
exposures.risk_note: Mixed: metabolic monitoring demand may grow, device pressure if GLP-1 reduces insulin need.

exposures.label: Insulet Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: Insulet makes Omnipod insulin delivery systems. GLP-1 adoption may reduce insulin dependency, creating volume pressure on insulin delivery devices.
exposures.risk_note: GLP-1 adoption may reduce insulin-dependent patient volumes over time.

exposures.label: UnitedHealth Group
exposures.reason_to_care: UnitedHealth faces higher drug costs from GLP-1 coverage expansion offset by potential long-term comorbidity cost savings. Direction is unclear; monitor for payer coverage policy changes.
exposures.risk_note: Higher drug costs vs long-term savings — mixed direction.

exposures.label: Mondelez International
exposures.reason_to_care: Mondelez sells calorie-dense snacks and chocolates. Reduced appetite from GLP-1 use creates structural volume pressure on snacking categories. Monitor-only negative exposure.
exposures.risk_note: Negative/mixed — monitor, not a directional trade.

exposures.label: Hershey Company
exposures.reason_to_care: Hershey's confectionery products face demand headwinds from GLP-1 appetite reduction and shifting consumer preferences toward metabolic health.
exposures.risk_note: Negative/mixed — monitor, not a directional trade.

exposures.label: Coca-Cola
exposures.reason_to_care: Coca-Cola's sugary beverages face long-term demand headwinds from the GLP-1 metabolic health trend and reduced appetite for high-calorie drinks.
exposures.risk_note: Negative/mixed — monitor, not a directional trade.

exposures.label: McDonald's Corp.
exposures.reason_to_care: McDonald's high-calorie menu faces potential volume headwinds from GLP-1 appetite reduction among obese/overweight demographics.
exposures.risk_note: Negative/mixed — monitor, not a directional trade.

exposures.label: Lockheed Martin
exposures.reason_to_care: Lockheed Martin is the world's largest defence contractor with F-35 jets, Patriot missiles, and hypersonic weapons programmes. NATO spending commitments and rising global defence budgets directly extend multi-year order backlogs.

exposures.label: RTX Corporation (Raytheon)
exposures.reason_to_care: RTX supplies Patriot/NASAMS air defence systems, Javelin missiles, and Pratt & Whitney engines. Munitions replenishment and air defence spending are structural tailwinds from NATO demand.

exposures.label: Northrop Grumman
exposures.reason_to_care: Northrop Grumman provides B-21 Raider bomber, ground-based strategic deterrent, and space systems. Rising defence budgets directly support multi-year backlog growth.

exposures.label: General Dynamics
exposures.reason_to_care: General Dynamics provides Stryker vehicles, Virginia-class submarines, and Gulfstream aircraft. Naval and ground defence spending drive backlog with submarine delivery schedule extending multi-year.

exposures.label: L3Harris Technologies
exposures.reason_to_care: L3Harris provides tactical radios, night vision, intelligence systems, and Electronic Warfare. NATO modernisation drives equipment upgrade cycles.

exposures.label: AeroVironment
exposures.reason_to_care: AeroVironment makes tactical UAVs and Switchblade loitering munitions. Conflict-driven demand and NATO munitions replenishment are direct revenue drivers.

exposures.label: Kratos Defense & Security
exposures.reason_to_care: Kratos produces drone target systems, tactical missiles, and satellite systems. Rising defence drone demand is a direct revenue driver.

exposures.label: Huntington Ingalls Industries
exposures.reason_to_care: Huntington Ingalls is the largest US military shipbuilder building Virginia-class submarines and CVN carriers. Multi-year government programme spending provides structural revenue visibility.

exposures.label: Rocket Lab
exposures.reason_to_care: Rocket Lab provides small satellite launches and spacecraft components. Military space reconnaissance demand creates upside in a growing commercial and government space launch market.
exposures.risk_note: Pre-profitability; launch failure risk exists.

exposures.label: Palantir Technologies
exposures.reason_to_care: Palantir provides AI data analytics platforms (Gotham, Foundry, AIP) for US and NATO defence agencies. Rising defence budgets support Palantir government segment revenue.

exposures.label: iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: ITA provides exposure to US aerospace and defence companies. Holdings are a discovery source for defence sector names.

exposures.label: SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: XAR provides broader exposure including mid-cap aerospace and defence names via the S&P TMI. Holdings serve as a candidate discovery source.

exposures.label: Palo Alto Networks
exposures.reason_to_care: Palo Alto Networks is a leading cybersecurity platform provider. Rising cyber risk and board-level security spend drive PANW's Next-Generation Security platform adoption across enterprise and government.

exposures.label: CrowdStrike Holdings
exposures.reason_to_care: CrowdStrike's Falcon platform is the leading endpoint detection and response (EDR/XDR) solution. AI-driven threat detection drives enterprise adoption growth and platform consolidation.

exposures.label: Fortinet
exposures.reason_to_care: Fortinet provides integrated security platforms including firewall, SASE, and SD-WAN. Enterprise security consolidation and network perimeter security spend are structural tailwinds.

exposures.label: Zscaler
exposures.reason_to_care: Zscaler's Zero Trust Exchange is the leading SASE platform. Cloud migration and remote-work security mandates drive ZS Zero Trust network access adoption.
exposures.risk_note: Growth/valuation-sensitive.

exposures.label: Cloudflare
exposures.reason_to_care: Cloudflare provides Zero Trust security and networking. AI-driven threat protection, DDoS mitigation, and secure access are structural enterprise demand drivers.
exposures.risk_note: Growth/valuation-sensitive.

exposures.label: Okta Inc.
exposures.reason_to_care: Okta is the leading identity and access management platform. Identity attacks are the most common breach vector per IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2025 Report.

exposures.label: CyberArk Software
exposures.reason_to_care: CyberArk specialises in Privileged Access Management (PAM). Breach costs and insider threat risks drive PAM adoption across enterprise and government.

exposures.label: First Trust Nasdaq Cybersecurity ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: CIBR provides basket exposure to cybersecurity companies. Holdings are a candidate discovery source for sector names.

exposures.label: ETFMG Prime Cyber Security ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: HACK provides diversified cybersecurity basket exposure including global security companies.

exposures.label: Applied Materials
exposures.reason_to_care: Applied Materials provides wafer fabrication equipment for semiconductor fabs. CHIPS Act investments and US fab construction by TSM, Intel, and Samsung directly drive capital equipment revenue.

exposures.label: Lam Research
exposures.reason_to_care: Lam Research provides etch and deposition equipment critical for every semiconductor fab. US fab construction drives equipment capital spend.

exposures.label: KLA Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: KLA provides process control and inspection equipment. Semiconductor fab yield improvement is a structural demand driver regardless of geography.

exposures.label: ASML Holding NV
exposures.reason_to_care: ASML is the sole supplier of EUV lithography equipment. Indispensable for any advanced semiconductor node fab globally. No alternative supplier exists.
exposures.risk_note: China export restrictions are a risk to revenue.

exposures.label: Rockwell Automation
exposures.reason_to_care: Rockwell Automation provides factory automation systems. Domestic manufacturing investment and CHIPS Act factory construction directly drive industrial automation demand.

exposures.label: Honeywell International
exposures.reason_to_care: Honeywell provides industrial automation, process controls, and building systems for manufacturing facilities. Diversified industrial exposure to domestic capex cycle.

exposures.label: EMCOR Group
exposures.reason_to_care: EMCOR provides industrial and mechanical construction for semiconductor fabs and factory buildouts. Growing US industrial construction backlog is a direct revenue driver.
exposures.risk_note: Note: also appears in AI power theme.

exposures.label: Nucor Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: Nucor produces steel for construction and manufacturing. Domestic infrastructure investment and reshoring support domestic steel demand.
exposures.risk_note: Cyclical — price and demand gates required.

exposures.label: Vulcan Materials
exposures.reason_to_care: Vulcan Materials provides aggregates and construction materials for infrastructure and industrial construction projects.
exposures.risk_note: Cyclical — price and demand gates required.

exposures.label: Global X U.S. Infrastructure Development ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: PAVE provides exposure to US companies involved in raw materials, heavy equipment, engineering and construction for infrastructure activity.

exposures.label: D.R. Horton
exposures.reason_to_care: D.R. Horton is the largest US homebuilder by volume. Mortgage rate relief directly drives buyer affordability and order recovery, particularly for first-time buyers who dominate DHI's volume.

exposures.label: Lennar Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: Lennar operates nationally with entry-level and move-up segments. Rate relief increases buyer qualification and reduces incentive costs, directly expanding gross margins.

exposures.label: PulteGroup
exposures.reason_to_care: PulteGroup's multi-brand strategy serves first-time, move-up, and active adult (Del Webb) buyers. Rate sensitivity is moderate compared to pure entry-level builders.

exposures.label: NVR Inc.
exposures.reason_to_care: NVR's asset-light lot-option model minimises land risk and provides strong returns on equity. Rate cuts improve buyer affordability with lower NVR-specific execution risk.

exposures.label: Builders FirstSource
exposures.reason_to_care: Builders FirstSource is the largest US supplier of structural building products to homebuilders. Revenue tied directly to housing starts and builder order volumes.

exposures.label: Owens Corning
exposures.reason_to_care: Owens Corning provides insulation and roofing materials directly tied to housing starts and the repair/remodel cycle.

exposures.label: Home Depot
exposures.reason_to_care: Home Depot benefits from housing turnover and remodel activity. Rate cuts drive existing home sales and pull-forward of deferred maintenance spending.

exposures.label: Lowe's Companies
exposures.reason_to_care: Lowe's benefits from repair and remodel cycles. Pro-customer segment provides more stable revenue than consumer-driven turnover projects.

exposures.label: Rocket Companies
exposures.reason_to_care: Rocket Companies is the leading US mortgage originator. Rate cuts directly drive refinance and purchase mortgage volumes — the primary Rocket revenue driver.
exposures.risk_note: High rate-path sensitivity and mortgage spread volatility.

exposures.label: Fidelity National Financial
exposures.reason_to_care: Fidelity National Financial provides title insurance. Housing transaction volumes directly drive title premium revenue.

exposures.label: iShares U.S. Home Construction ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: ITB tracks US residential construction and related industries. Holdings focused on homebuilders and direct suppliers.

exposures.label: SPDR S&P Homebuilders ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: XHB includes homebuilders plus building products, furnishings, retail, and appliances. Broader than ITB, capturing the full housing supply chain.

exposures.label: American Water Works
exposures.reason_to_care: American Water Works is the largest US investor-owned water utility. Infrastructure investment and regulatory rate-case recovery directly support earnings growth.

exposures.label: Essential Utilities
exposures.reason_to_care: Essential Utilities provides water and natural gas utility services. Water infrastructure investment drives regulated rate base growth and capital recovery.

exposures.label: Xylem Inc.
exposures.reason_to_care: Xylem provides water technology including pumps, smart meters, and treatment systems. Water infrastructure investment directly drives Xylem revenue across utilities and industrial customers.

exposures.label: Pentair plc
exposures.reason_to_care: Pentair provides water treatment and fluid control systems for industrial, residential, and commercial applications.

exposures.label: Itron Inc.
exposures.reason_to_care: Itron provides smart metering and network solutions for water and energy utilities. Smart meter replacement cycles and infrastructure modernisation drive demand.

exposures.label: Ecolab Inc.
exposures.reason_to_care: Ecolab provides water treatment, hygiene, and cleaning solutions to industrial and commercial customers. Industrial water efficiency and treatment demand are structural drivers.

exposures.label: Invesco Water Resources ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: PHO seeks exposure to companies that create products to conserve and purify water. Holdings provide a discovery source for water sector names.

exposures.label: First Trust Water ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: FIW provides broader water-sector basket exposure across utilities, equipment, and treatment companies.

exposures.label: Freeport-McMoRan
exposures.reason_to_care: Freeport-McMoRan is the world's largest publicly traded copper producer. Electrification, data-centre infrastructure, and grid expansion create structural multi-decade copper demand growth that supply additions cannot easily match.

exposures.label: Southern Copper
exposures.reason_to_care: Southern Copper operates large-scale Latin American mines with among the highest copper revenue purity of any listed miner.

exposures.label: Teck Resources
exposures.reason_to_care: Teck Resources has growing copper exposure through Quebrada Blanca (QB2) and Carmen de Andacollo mines following its energy sale.

exposures.label: Albemarle Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: Albemarle is the world's largest lithium producer. EV battery demand and energy storage growth drive lithium demand — separate from copper bucket.
exposures.risk_note: Requires battery/EV driver activation — not auto-included from electrification alone.

exposures.label: Sociedad Química y Minera
exposures.reason_to_care: SQM is a leading Chilean lithium and specialty chemicals producer. Lithium carbonate and hydroxide production serve the EV battery supply chain.
exposures.risk_note: Chile government regulation and lithium price cycle are key risks.

exposures.label: MP Materials
exposures.reason_to_care: MP Materials operates the only rare earth mining and processing facility in the US at Mountain Pass, California. China rare earth export restrictions are a direct supply-chain risk driver.
exposures.risk_note: Geopolitical supply-chain driver required for full activation.

exposures.label: Global X Copper Miners ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: Global X Copper Miners ETF provides basket exposure to global copper mining companies.

exposures.label: iShares Copper and Metals Mining ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: iShares Copper and Metals Mining ETF tracks global copper and metal-ore mining companies.

exposures.label: Global X Lithium & Battery Tech ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: LIT provides basket exposure to lithium producers and battery manufacturers in a single ETF.

exposures.label: SPDR Gold Shares
exposures.reason_to_care: SPDR Gold Shares is the largest gold ETF by assets under management. Provides direct bullion price exposure without operating company or mining risk.

exposures.label: iShares Gold Trust
exposures.reason_to_care: iShares Gold Trust provides lower-cost gold bullion exposure than GLD with a similar physically-backed structure.

exposures.label: Newmont Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: Newmont is the world's largest gold miner. Higher gold prices directly amplify earnings due to fixed-cost leverage in mining operations.
exposures.risk_note: Mine cost and jurisdiction risk required.

exposures.label: Barrick Gold
exposures.reason_to_care: Barrick Gold is a major global gold and copper miner. Gold price leverage plus emerging copper upside from growing copper operations.

exposures.label: Agnico Eagle Mines
exposures.reason_to_care: Agnico Eagle is a Canadian major gold producer with mines in stable jurisdictions (Canada, Finland, Australia). Lower geopolitical risk than African or South American miners.

exposures.label: Franco-Nevada Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: Franco-Nevada is the world's premier gold royalty/streaming company. No operating costs means pure gold-price leverage. Different risk profile from miners — revenue without mine execution risk.

exposures.label: Wheaton Precious Metals
exposures.reason_to_care: Wheaton Precious Metals is the largest silver/gold streaming company. Streaming model provides low-cost precious metals exposure with a different risk profile from WPM vs NEM.

exposures.label: Royal Gold Inc.
exposures.reason_to_care: Royal Gold provides royalty and streaming financing to mining companies. Lower cost structure than operators means higher gold-price leverage per dollar invested.

exposures.label: VanEck Gold Miners ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: VanEck Gold Miners ETF provides exposure to senior and mid-tier gold mining companies. Higher beta to gold price than bullion ETFs.

exposures.label: VanEck Junior Gold Miners ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: VanEck Junior Gold Miners ETF provides higher-beta exposure to junior and mid-tier gold miners. Higher volatility than GDX.

exposures.label: iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: iShares Bitcoin Trust is the largest spot Bitcoin ETF by assets. Provides direct Bitcoin price exposure via SEC-approved ETP structure, approved January 10, 2024.

exposures.label: Fidelity Wise Origin Bitcoin Fund
exposures.reason_to_care: Fidelity Wise Origin Bitcoin Fund is a spot Bitcoin ETF with direct Bitcoin exposure and competitive fee structure.

exposures.label: Grayscale Bitcoin Trust ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: Grayscale Bitcoin Trust converted from closed-end trust to an ETF. Older structure with higher management fees than newer spot Bitcoin ETPs.
exposures.risk_note: Higher fees than IBIT or FBTC.

exposures.label: Coinbase Global
exposures.reason_to_care: Coinbase is the largest US crypto exchange. Revenue is directly tied to crypto trading volumes, custody assets, and USDC stablecoin interest income. Transaction and subscription revenue grow with crypto market cycles.
exposures.risk_note: Revenue sensitive to trading volume, custody, subscriptions, and regulation.

exposures.label: Robinhood Markets
exposures.reason_to_care: Robinhood offers crypto trading alongside equities. Crypto volume expansion benefits transaction revenue but crypto is one of several revenue streams.

exposures.label: Strategy (MicroStrategy)
exposures.reason_to_care: Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) holds over 500,000 Bitcoin on its balance sheet. Stock trades as a leveraged Bitcoin price proxy. Balance-sheet Bitcoin proxy with operating leverage.
exposures.risk_note: High volatility, leverage, and valuation controls required.

exposures.label: MARA Holdings
exposures.reason_to_care: MARA Holdings (formerly Marathon Digital) is the largest US Bitcoin miner by hashrate. Mining revenue tied to hash price, energy costs, and Bitcoin holdings on balance sheet.
exposures.risk_note: Mining economics, hash price, energy cost, and balance-sheet risk required.

exposures.label: Riot Platforms
exposures.reason_to_care: Riot Platforms is a major US Bitcoin miner. Mining profitability is tied to hash price, energy economics, and operational efficiency.
exposures.risk_note: Mining economics risk.

exposures.label: CleanSpark
exposures.reason_to_care: CleanSpark is a Bitcoin miner focused on sustainable energy mining. Hash rate growth and energy cost management are key value drivers.
exposures.risk_note: Mining economics risk.

exposures.label: Amplify Transformational Data Sharing ETF
exposures.reason_to_care: BLOK provides exposure to companies actively involved in blockchain technology and digital asset infrastructure.

exposures.label: Oklo Inc.
exposures.reason_to_care: Oklo is developing advanced fission power plants (Aurora powerhouses). Pre-revenue stage with NRC licensing review pending.
exposures.risk_note: Pre-revenue; NRC review and commercial deployment timeline uncertain.

exposures.label: Viking Therapeutics
exposures.reason_to_care: Viking Therapeutics' VK2735 is in Phase 2/3 clinical development for obesity. Catalyst-driven with binary trial risk.
exposures.risk_note: Pre-approval; binary clinical trial risk.

exposures.label: Amgen Inc.
exposures.reason_to_care: Amgen's MariTide (AMG 133) is in Phase 2 obesity development. Not a direct GLP-1 revenue leader — catalyst-driven pipeline name.
exposures.risk_note: Clinical stage pipeline exposure — not direct drug revenue.

exposures.label: AST SpaceMobile
exposures.reason_to_care: AST SpaceMobile is building a direct-to-device satellite broadband constellation using low-Earth-orbit satellites. Commercial launch milestones and government connectivity contracts connect it to the broader space infrastructure and dual-use satellite communications theme.
exposures.risk_note: High cash burn pre-revenue. Constellation build-out risk. Dilution risk at current scale.

exposures.label: Applied Digital Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: Applied Digital operates high-performance computing data centres purpose-built for AI workloads and cryptocurrency mining. HPC cloud revenue from AI hosting is a growing share of revenue as hyperscalers seek alternative GPU compute capacity.
exposures.risk_note: Dual AI/crypto revenue mix creates volatility. Balance sheet leverage is a risk.

exposures.label: Applied Digital Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: Applied Digital mines Bitcoin and operates high-density compute infrastructure. BTC price and network hashrate directly affect mining economics and hosting demand.
exposures.risk_note: Dual AI/crypto revenue mix — BTC price and halving cycle drive revenue swings.

exposures.label: Iris Energy
exposures.reason_to_care: Iris Energy operates renewable-powered data centres for AI HPC cloud services and Bitcoin mining. The company is actively converting Bitcoin mining capacity to GPU AI compute hosting, directly capturing the AI infrastructure buildout.
exposures.risk_note: Revenue split between AI hosting and BTC mining creates regime sensitivity. Capacity build-out capex is significant.

exposures.label: Iris Energy
exposures.reason_to_care: Iris Energy is a renewable-powered Bitcoin miner. BTC price and halving cycle directly affect mining revenue. The company also has HPC/AI hosting capacity providing revenue diversification.
exposures.risk_note: BTC price sensitivity. Transition to AI hosting reduces but does not eliminate crypto exposure.

exposures.label: IBM Corporation
exposures.reason_to_care: IBM WatsonX and hybrid cloud platform (Red Hat OpenShift) serve enterprise AI adoption. IBM is one of the few large-caps with a dedicated enterprise AI platform positioned for on-premises and regulated-industry deployment where hyperscaler public cloud is restricted.
exposures.risk_note: Mature business with declining legacy segments. AI thesis depends on WatsonX enterprise adoption rate.

exposures.label: ServiceNow Inc.
exposures.reason_to_care: ServiceNow is embedding AI agents (Now Assist) across its enterprise workflow platform. AI feature attach rates drive premium pricing and NRR expansion. ServiceNow is uniquely positioned as the AI orchestration layer for enterprise IT, HR, and operations workflows — it monetises AI adoption without GPU spend.
exposures.risk_note: High valuation. Competitive pressure from Microsoft Copilot and Salesforce Einstein on workflow automation.

exposures.label: Zeta Global Holdings
exposures.reason_to_care: Zeta Global uses AI to power its marketing data platform. AI-driven customer intelligence and campaign personalisation are embedded in its revenue model. This is an indirect AI beneficiary — not a compute/infrastructure play but an AI-enabled software platform.
exposures.risk_note: Adtech exposure to digital ad spend cycles. Not a pure AI infrastructure play — thematic link is indirect.
